description: Bounded shared-fixture fidelity policy for rhwp-mcp vs external hwp-mcp evidence. This is not a full corpus benchmark; it fixes a small mixed set of common-document signals that must remain true before claiming representative external fidelity.

cases.name: government_same_edit_hwpx
cases.label: Government HWPX same-edit output
cases.intent: Localized government-form edits should preserve page count, text/profile/style signatures, render geometry, and pixels; raw package differences may only be lexical/XML serialization drift.

cases.name: business_same_edit_hwpx
cases.label: Business overview HWPX same-edit output
cases.intent: Compact business-document edits should preserve text/profile/style/render output; raw structural drift is limited to line-segment preservation.

cases.name: foreign_investment_same_edit_hwpx
cases.label: Foreign-investment HWPX same-edit output
cases.intent: Long-form press-release edits should keep pages, page-1 and target-page pixels, render geometry, text/profile/style signatures, and source/local/external line segment counts aligned; raw package drift is limited to run-count differences.

cases.name: chart_ole_original_and_semantic_surface
cases.label: Chart/OLE original render and local semantic edit surface
cases.intent: The shared chart page should render equally before semantic edits, while local rhwp-mcp must expose chart semantic edit/save/reopen capability that external hwp-mcp currently lacks.

cases.name: hwp_binary_read_render
cases.label: HWP binary read/render shared fixture
cases.intent: Both servers should read and render an authored HWP binary document with a shared body phrase; local extraction should remain broad. Page-1 visual drift is allowed only as bounded font fallback/rasterization drift, not layout or text loss.
